review.summary: The opaque-ticket refactor sensibly minimizes public dispatch metadata and adds strong reservation-artifact validation, redirect token stripping, and replay defense. However the workflow's global concurrency group (u1-claude-global-review) claimed in README and asserted by tests is not present in the shown workflow diff, so the anti-double-consumption serialization guarantee is unproven. Additionally the concurrency serialization does not prevent a second run consuming the same ticket after the first completes; replay defense rests solely on the run-ledger count==1 check, which has a TOCTOU window against concurrent runs. Publish now uses source_token instead of the review token, changing credential semantics.

findings.finding: README and test_workflow_serializes_opaque_ticket_consumption assert 'group: u1-claude-global-review' and 'cancel-in-progress: false', but the workflow diff never adds any concurrency block. The claimed serialization that stops two copies consuming one ticket is unproven by the supplied diff; test may pass on unshown workflow content or fail. Missing fail-closed serialization for ticket reuse.

Replay prevention relies on verify_run_budget counting current_runs with matching opaque title ==1. Two concurrent executor runs for the same ticket can both observe count==1 before either registers, a TOCTOU race. Without proven workflow-level concurrency (see above), the run-ledger check does not fail-closed against concurrent double dispatch of one ticket.

publish now posts the comment using source_token (SEASON2_REVIEW_TOKEN path renamed source_token) rather than the prior dedicated review token; the diff removes the separate 'token' variable and uses source_token for both content read and issue comment POST. This broadens the source token's write scope to PR comments, a credential-scope change not justified in the change description.

resolve_dispatch_ticket resolves pr_number via an open-PR head/base filter expecting exactly one result, but head_sha binding to that PR is only cross-checked through github_resolved.current_head_sha inside the artifact, not re-verified live against the resolved PR's head at publish time here; drift between reservation snapshot and live PR head is caught only later by verify_remote_state.

README asserts 'protected-runtime availability must be verified before any future Fable workflow is activated' and that U1 always uses Opus 4.8; this is documentation-only policy with no enforcing code in the diff, so the model-routing claim is unsupported by tests.

_SafeRedirectHandler strips Authorization on redirect and rejects non-https; api_bytes enforces api.github.com origin and a 100KB cap; parse_reservation_archive rejects multi-member, oversized, encrypted, or directory entries. These reduce token-leak and zip-abuse risk and are exercised by tests.

verification: Public dispatch now accepts only ticket_id; tests assert pilot_id/pr_number/head_sha/request_id removed from inputs and run-name.
validate_ticket rejects non-positive and bool ticket IDs (INVALID).
parse_reservation_archive rejects extra members, non-JSON, oversized, directory, and encrypted entries.
validate_reservation_context binds pilot, branch, paths, side effects, policy SHA, lease, and per-family/per-pilot budgets to the ticket.
verify_run_budget denies non-opaque run titles and denies when opaque ticket title count != 1 (replay denial test).
api_bytes strips Authorization on redirect and enforces https + api.github.com origin with size cap.
requirement_coverage: Credential exposure: partially covered via redirect token stripping and origin pinning; but source_token scope broadened for publish.
Budget bypass: per-family and per-pilot ledger checks enforced in reservation context and run budget.
Fail-closed dispatch: unproven for concurrent ticket consumption due to missing workflow concurrency block in diff.
Tests proving claims: workflow serialization test asserts content not present in the shown workflow diff.
Public metadata minimization: covered; only opaque ticket exposed.
residual_risk: Concurrent double-consumption of one ticket if the claimed workflow concurrency group is absent at deploy time.
Source token now able to post PR comments, widening blast radius if leaked.
Reservation snapshot vs live PR head drift relies on downstream verify_remote_state, not re-checked in ticket resolution.
